response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a well-rounded full-stack and systems engineer with 15+ years of broad experience, but his profile fundamentally mismatches every mandatory requirement for this role. His C++ exposure is minimal — roughly six months at National Instruments plus a bachelor thesis project — with no demonstrated Windows-platform C++ work. SQL experience is listed as a general skill with no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth. He has no Fixed Income, Fixed Income Derivatives, or traditional Capital Markets/Investment Banking domain knowledge; his sole finance-adjacent role (RIDE Capital) was a TypeScript/GraphQL fintech startup unrelated to capital-markets systems. The role demands a deep C++/Windows specialist with financial-domain expertise; this candidate is a generalist operating in an orthogonal stack.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software engineering across diverse languages and domains demonstrates strong adaptability
CI/CD, Java, and scripting experience partially satisfies the optional desirable skills list
Public talks and workshops demonstrate strong communication and knowledge-sharing ability
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months professional plus a thesis project, far below the 5–10 year mandatory requirement and with no Windows-platform evidence
SQL Server and Oracle are absent from the resume; SQL appears only as a line-item skill with no demonstrated depth
No Fixed Income, Derivatives, Capital Markets, or Investment Banking domain exposure of any kind

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ depth and Windows experience
No capital markets or fixed income domain knowledge
